subject: science
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: soil is composed of rock, organic material, water and air with measurable characteristics. Georgia S3E1b: plan and carry out investigations to describe properties (color, texture, capacity to retain water, plant-growth support) of soils and soil types (sand, clay, loam).

subject: science
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: rocks have specific observable characteristics that allow them to be sorted and compared. Georgia S3E1a: ask questions and analyze data to classify rocks by physical attributes (color, texture, luster, hardness) using simple tests.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade K). Ohio: air and water are also nonliving Earth resources alongside soil and rock. Georgia SKE2a: ask questions to identify and describe earth materials—soil, rocks, water, and air—covering the same nonliving resources at an earlier grade.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 6). Ohio: renewable energy (wind, water, solar) can be replenished while nonrenewable energy (natural gas, coal, oil) is finite. Georgia S6E6a: determine the differences between renewable/sustainable energy resources (hydro, solar, wind) and nonrenewable resources (fossil fuels: oil, coal, natural gas).

subject: science
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: reducing resource use, recycling and reusing can help conserve limited resources. Georgia S3L2b: research and communicate solutions such as conservation of resources and recycling of materials to protect plants and animals.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 6). Ohio: some Earth resources become limited due to overuse and contamination and can be conserved. Georgia S6E6b: design and evaluate solutions for sustaining the quality and supply of natural resources such as water, soil, and air.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 2). Ohio: matter can change from one state to another by heating or cooling. Georgia S2P1c: provide evidence that some changes in matter caused by heating or cooling can be reversed and some are irreversible.

subject: science
rationale: Same-grade partial overlap. Ohio names heat, electrical, light, sound and magnetic energy as forms of energy. Georgia S3P1a addresses only one of these—identifying sources of heat energy (sunlight, friction, burning)—so the overlap is real but incomplete.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 5). Ohio: individual organisms inherit many traits from their parents. Georgia S5L2b: ask questions to compare and contrast inherited and acquired physical traits.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade K). Ohio: offspring resemble their parents and each other. Georgia SKL2c: make observations to identify the similarities and differences of offspring to their parents and to other members of the same species.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 5). Ohio: some behavioral traits are learned through interactions with the environment and are not inherited. Georgia S5L2a: ask questions to compare and contrast instincts and learned behaviors.

subject: science
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: plants and animals have physical features and characteristics associated with their environments that influence survival. Georgia S3L1b: construct an explanation of how external features and adaptations allow animals to survive in their habitat.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 7). Ohio: inherited-trait differences give some individuals an advantage in surviving and reproducing. Georgia S7L5b: describe how genetic variation and environmental factors influence the probability of survival and reproduction of a species.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 2). Ohio: the details of the life cycle differ for different organisms. Georgia S2L1d: develop models to illustrate the unique and diverse life cycles of organisms other than humans.

subject: science
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 2). Ohio: organisms have life cycles through which they grow, reproduce, die and decay. Georgia S2L1a: ask questions to determine the sequence of the life cycle of common animals such as a mammal, bird, amphibian, and insect.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: daily life is influenced by the agriculture, industry and natural resources in different communities. Georgia SS3G3c: explain how the physical geography of the New England, Mid-Atlantic, and Southern colonies helped determine economic activities.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 8). Ohio: systems of transportation move people, products and ideas from place to place. Georgia SS8E1a: evaluate how the Interstate Highway System, airport, deepwater ports, and railroads interact to support the exchange of goods and services domestically and internationally.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade K). Ohio: communities may include diverse cultural groups. Georgia SSKG1: describe the diversity of American culture by explaining the customs and celebrations of various families and communities.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: members of local communities have rights and responsibilities. Georgia SS3CG2a: explain the necessity of respecting the rights of others and promoting the common good.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 5). Ohio: members of local communities have rights and responsibilities. Georgia SS5CG1a: explain the responsibilities of a citizen.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: individuals make the community a better place by taking action to solve problems and promote the common good. Georgia SS3CG2b: explain why it is important for citizens in a democratic society to participate in civic life (staying informed, voting, volunteering, communicating with public officials).

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 2). Ohio: laws are rules that apply to all people, promote order and security, and protect rights. Georgia SS2CG1: define the concept of government and the need for rules and laws.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: governments have authority to make and enforce laws. Georgia SS3CG1c: state the main responsibility of each branch—executive (enforcing laws), legislative (making laws), judicial (determining if laws are fair).

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 8). Ohio: the structure of local governments may differ from one community to another. Georgia SS8CG6a: explain the origins and purposes of city, county, and special-purpose governments in Georgia.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 4). Ohio: both positive and negative incentives affect individuals' choices and behaviors. Georgia SS4E1b: explain how price incentives affect people's behavior and choices; GA limits this to price incentives, so the overlap is clear but narrower.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 2). Ohio: individuals must make decisions because of scarcity of resources, and making a decision involves a trade-off. Georgia SS2E1: explain that because of scarcity, people must make choices that result in opportunity costs.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: making a decision involves a trade-off due to scarcity. Georgia SS3E4: explain the concept of opportunity cost as it relates to making a saving or spending choice.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: a consumer's wants are satisfied by using goods and services while a producer makes goods and provides services. Georgia SS3E3a: describe the interdependence of consumers and producers.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 1). Ohio distinguishes consumers from producers. Georgia SS1E3: describe how people are both producers and consumers.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Same-grade match. Ohio: a market is where buyers and sellers exchange goods and services. Georgia SS3E3b: describe how goods and services are allocated by price in the marketplace.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 2). Ohio: making decisions involves weighing costs and benefits. Georgia SS2E4: describe the costs and benefits of personal saving and spending choices.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 4). Ohio: a budget is a plan to help people make personal economic decisions and become more financially responsible. Georgia SS4E2: identify the elements of a personal budget (income, expenditures, saving) and explain why personal spending and saving decisions are important.

subject: social_studies
rationale: Cross-grade (GA grade 6). Ohio: a budget is a plan to make personal economic decisions for the present and future. Georgia SS6E13b: understand that a budget is a tool to plan the spending and saving of income.
